symptoms.symptom_name: Recent ingestion of St John's Wort and high-dose turmeric supplements alongside apixaban

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: St John's Wort - apixaban interaction reducing anticoagulation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: St John's Wort is an established CYP3A4/P-gp inducer that lowers apixaban levels - the core reconciliation issue of the encounter.
The feared exposure is the trigger for the acute anxiety and somatic symptoms.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpitations (fluttering chest sensation), settling with rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute health anxiety with benign somatic palpitations
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Transient, settling palpitations tracking anxiety with a stable regular pulse favour a benign catecholamine-driven cause.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpitations are non-specific; must be screened but are not sustained or fast here.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute health anxiety with benign somatic palpitations
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific lightheadedness common with hyperventilation/anxiety; no syncope or sustained hypotension.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute anxiety / fear of a catastrophic drug interaction

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute health anxiety with benign somatic palpitations
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The dominant driver of the presentation; the somatic symptoms are its manifestation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heart rate 88 and regular (nurse-relayed); no sustained tachyarrhythmia

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A controlled regular rate of 88 excludes RVR; the AI can only rely on the nurse-relayed value, not an independent measurement.

symptoms.symptom_name: No bleeding signs (no petechiae, epistaxis, haematuria, melaena or new bruising)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of bleeding features lowers the probability of major haemorrhage, but a remote assessment cannot fully exclude occult bleeding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal, symmetrical neurological findings (alert and oriented, no focal deficit, speech intact)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of focal deficit and preserved speech argue against acute stroke; formal neuro exam is a clinician task.

investigation_details.expected_result: Not performed in this encounter; would quantify the degree of anticoagulation but is not required for a single-dose exposure with no bleeding.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: None for immediate management; the interaction is managed by reconciliation and monitoring, not by an acute assay.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: St John's Wort - apixaban interaction reducing anticoagulation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anti-Xa levels are not routinely used to manage a single supplement exposure; reconciliation and clinical monitoring are the levers.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Medication reconciliation against the facility chart (identify ingredients, secure supplements)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The core clinical action - confirm the St John's Wort + turmeric ingredients, secure the supplements with the on-site nurse, and reconcile against apixaban/metoprolol.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine coagulation profile / apixaban anti-Xa assay
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated for a single supplement dose with no bleeding features; over-ordering it medicalises a benign encounter.
